messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
The collisions between ants are analyzed by computing successive meeting times from the relative velocities and positions. (Each ant retains a constant speed magnitude at all times and merely reverses its direction of travel upon any collision.)

- Ants A and B are initially approaching each other. Their separation is \(30\) cm and their relative speed is \(4\) cm/s, so they collide after
  \[
  t_1 = \frac{30}{4} = \frac{15}{2}\ \text{s}
  \]
  at position \(65/2\) cm. Immediately after the collision the velocities are \(v_A = -3\) cm/s and \(v_B = +1\) cm/s. In the same interval ant C has moved to position \(65\) cm.

- Ants B and C are now approaching each other. Their separation is \(65/2\) cm and their relative speed is \(3\) cm/s, so they collide after an additional
  \[
  t_2 = \frac{65/2}{3} = \frac{65}{6}\ \text{s}
  \]
  (i.e., at absolute time \(55/3\) s) at position \(130/3\) cm. At this exact instant ant A reaches the left end of the rod and falls off. Immediately after the collision the velocities are \(v_B = -1\) cm/s and \(v_C = +2\) cm/s.

- The two remaining ants are now moving away from each other, so no further collisions occur.
  - Ant C travels the remaining \(170/3\) cm to the right end at speed \(2\) cm/s and falls off after an additional \(85/3\) s (absolute time \(140/3\) s).
  - Ant B travels the remaining \(130/3\) cm to the left end at speed \(1\) cm/s and falls off after an additional \(130/3\) s (absolute time \(185/3\) s).

The three fall-off times are therefore \(55/3\), \(140/3\) and \(185/3\). The last of these is \(185/3\).

ANSWER: 185/3
